subject: general-knowledge
description: Secret Intelligence Agencies MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: KAPO (Internal Security Service) is the intelligence agency of which country?
mcqs.explanation: KAPO (Kaitsepolitseiamet) is Estonia's Internal Security Service, responsible for domestic counter-intelligence, terrorism prevention, and constitutional security. It is often compared to Latvia's SAB or Lithuania's VSD.

mcqs.q: The KGB was the primary security and intelligence agency for which nation?
mcqs.explanation: The KGB (Komitet Gosudarstvennoy Bezopasnosti) was the primary security and intelligence agency of the Soviet Union, dissolved in 1991. Its successors are FSB (domestic) and SVR (foreign) in Russia. Vladimir Putin was a KGB officer.

mcqs.q: DRM (Directorate of Military Intelligence) belongs to which country?
mcqs.explanation: DRM (Direction du Renseignement Militaire) is France's Directorate of Military Intelligence. France also has DGSE for foreign intelligence and DGSI for domestic counter-intelligence.

mcqs.q: BfV (Federal Office for the Protection of the Constitution) is the domestic intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: BfV (Bundesamt für Verfassungsschutz) is Germany's domestic counter-intelligence agency. Germany's foreign intelligence agency is BND (Bundesnachrichtendienst). Germany also has MAD for military counter-intelligence.

mcqs.q: LfV refers to the State Offices for the Protection of the Constitution in which country?
mcqs.explanation: LfV (Landesämter für Verfassungsschutz) are the state-level offices for the Protection of the Constitution in Germany. Each of Germany's 16 federal states has its own LfV working alongside the federal BfV.

mcqs.q: BND (Federal Intelligence Service) is the foreign intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: BND (Bundesnachrichtendienst — Federal Intelligence Service) is Germany's foreign intelligence agency, founded in 1956 and headquartered in Berlin. It is Germany's equivalent of the CIA.

mcqs.q: MAD is the Military Counter-Intelligence Service of:
mcqs.explanation: MAD (Militärischer Abschirmdienst — Military Counter-Intelligence Service) is Germany's military security agency, protecting the Bundeswehr (German armed forces) from espionage and extremism.

mcqs.q: NIS (National Intelligence Service) is the intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: NIS (Εθνική Υπηρεσία Πληροφοριών) is Greece's primary intelligence agency, handling both domestic and foreign intelligence. It should not be confused with South Korea's NIS (National Intelligence Service) or Kenya's NIS.

mcqs.q: NBH (National Security Office) is the intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: NBH (Nemzetbiztonsági Hivatal — National Security Office) is Hungary's domestic intelligence and counter-intelligence agency. Hungary's military intelligence is handled by separately structured defence intelligence organizations.

mcqs.q: GRLS (Police/Analysis Unit) is a small intelligence unit located in:
mcqs.explanation: GRLS (Greiningardeild ríkislögreglustjóra — Police Analysis Unit) is Iceland's small police intelligence unit. As a small Nordic nation with no military, Iceland maintains a limited intelligence apparatus.

mcqs.q: DST (Direction de la Surveillance du Territoire) is an intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: DST (Direction de la Surveillance du Territoire) was Morocco's counter-intelligence and domestic security agency. Note: France also had a DST (now merged into DGSI), so the country context matters in MCQs.

mcqs.q: BIN (Badan Intelijen Negara) is the state intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: BIN (Badan Intelijen Negara — State Intelligence Agency) is Indonesia's national intelligence agency, reorganized under this name in 2001. Indonesia also has BAIS for military intelligence.

mcqs.q: GSD (General Security Directorate) was an intelligence agency in:
mcqs.explanation: GSD (General Security Directorate) was an intelligence agency in Iraq during the Saddam Hussein era. Iraq's intelligence apparatus was extensively reorganized after the 2003 US-led invasion.

mcqs.q: G2 is the military intelligence branch of:
mcqs.explanation: G2 is the military intelligence branch of Ireland's Defence Forces, responsible for gathering and analyzing intelligence relevant to national security. Ireland uses NATO-style staff designations (G2 for intelligence).

mcqs.q: Mossad (Institute for Intelligence and Special Tasks) is the national intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: Mossad is Israel's national intelligence agency for foreign affairs and covert operations, founded in 1949. Its motto is 'By deception thou shalt do war.' It is headquartered in Tel Aviv and considered one of the world's most effective intelligence agencies.

mcqs.q: Shin Bet (Shabak) is the internal security service of:
mcqs.explanation: Shin Bet (Shabak — Israel Security Agency) is Israel's domestic counter-intelligence and internal security service, founded in 1948. It handles counter-terrorism within Israel and the occupied territories. Mossad handles foreign intelligence; Aman handles military intelligence.

mcqs.q: Aman is the military intelligence directorate of:
mcqs.explanation: Aman (Agaf HaModi'in — Military Intelligence Directorate) is the Israeli Defense Forces' military intelligence branch. Together with Mossad (foreign) and Shin Bet (domestic), it forms Israel's three main intelligence services.

mcqs.q: AISI (Agenzia Informazioni e Sicurezza Interna) is the internal security agency of:
mcqs.explanation: AISI (Agenzia Informazioni e Sicurezza Interna) is Italy's internal security agency for counter-intelligence and counter-terrorism. Italy's foreign intelligence agency is AISE (Agenzia Informazioni e Sicurezza Esterna).

mcqs.q: AISE (Agenzia Informazioni e Sicurezza Esterna) is the foreign intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: AISE is Italy's foreign intelligence service — equivalent to the CIA. Its domestic counterpart is AISI. Both were created by Italian Law No. 124 of 2007, replacing the former SISMI and SISDE.

mcqs.q: CIRO (Cabinet Intelligence and Research Office) is the primary intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: CIRO (Naikaku Jouhou Chousa Shitsu — Cabinet Intelligence and Research Office) is Japan's primary intelligence agency, under the Cabinet Secretariat. Japan also has DIH (Defense Intelligence Headquarters) for military intelligence.

mcqs.q: DIH (Defense Intelligence Headquarters) operates in:
mcqs.explanation: DIH (Defense Intelligence Headquarters) is Japan's military intelligence organization, established in 1997 under the Ministry of Defense. It coordinates intelligence from Japan's Ground, Maritime, and Air Self-Defense Forces.

mcqs.q: GID (General Intelligence Department) is the intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: GID (General Intelligence Department), also known as Mukhabarat, is Jordan's primary intelligence agency. It is considered one of the most effective intelligence services in the Arab world and cooperates closely with Western agencies.

mcqs.q: NIS (National Intelligence Service) is the primary intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: NIS (National Intelligence Service) is Kenya's primary intelligence agency, responsible for both domestic and foreign intelligence. Kenya is a strategic intelligence partner for Western nations in East Africa.

mcqs.q: SAB (Constitution Protection Bureau) is the intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: SAB (Satversmes Aizsardzības Birojs — Constitution Protection Bureau) is Latvia's domestic intelligence and security service. The three Baltic states have similarly structured agencies: Estonia's KAPO, Latvia's SAB, and Lithuania's VSD.

mcqs.q: Mukhabarat el-Jamahiriya was the national intelligence service of:
mcqs.explanation: Mukhabarat el-Jamahiriya was Libya's national intelligence service under Colonel Muammar Gaddafi's regime. After Gaddafi's fall in 2011, Libya's intelligence infrastructure was largely dismantled and reorganized.

mcqs.q: The Royal Intelligence Corps is a military intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: The Royal Intelligence Corps (Rejimen Risik DiRaja) is Malaysia's military intelligence agency, part of the Malaysian Armed Forces. Malaysia also maintains civilian intelligence through its Special Branch.

mcqs.q: DGED (Direction Générale des Études et de la Documentation) is the intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: DGED (Direction Générale des Études et de la Documentation) is Morocco's foreign intelligence agency. Morocco's domestic intelligence counterpart is DST (Direction de la Surveillance du Territoire).

mcqs.q: SVR is the Foreign Intelligence Service of:
mcqs.explanation: SVR (Sluzhba Vneshney Razvedki — Foreign Intelligence Service) is Russia's foreign intelligence agency, created in 1991 as one of the KGB's successor organizations. It is Russia's CIA equivalent, while FSB handles domestic intelligence.

mcqs.q: FSB is the principal security agency and successor to the KGB in:
mcqs.explanation: FSB (Federalnaya Sluzhba Bezopasnosti — Federal Security Service) is Russia's principal domestic security agency, the main KGB successor. Founded in 1995 and headquartered at Lubyanka, Moscow. Vladimir Putin served as FSB Director (1998–1999) before becoming President.

mcqs.q: NDS (National Directorate of Security) was the primary intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: NDS (National Directorate of Security) was Afghanistan's primary intelligence agency under the Western-backed government. After the Taliban takeover in August 2021, it was replaced by the GDI (General Directorate of Intelligence).

mcqs.q: RAMA was an intelligence organization historically associated with:
mcqs.explanation: RAMA was an intelligence organization historically associated with Afghanistan during the Soviet-backed communist government period (1978–1992). It operated alongside KHAD, Afghanistan's KGB-modelled secret police.

mcqs.q: SHISH is the State Intelligence Service of:
mcqs.explanation: SHISH (Shërbimi Informativ Shtetëror — State Intelligence Service) is Albania's national intelligence agency, responsible for both foreign and domestic intelligence operations.

mcqs.q: DIS (Department of Intelligence and Security) is the intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: DIS (Department of Intelligence and Security) is Algeria's national intelligence agency. Algeria's intelligence apparatus has undergone significant restructuring, including the dissolution of the powerful DRS (Département du Renseignement et de la Sécurité) in 2016.

mcqs.q: NSS is the National Security Service of:
mcqs.explanation: NSS (Azgayin Anvanakhmbagutyun — National Security Service) is Armenia's primary intelligence and security agency, a successor to the KGB in Armenia following the Soviet Union's dissolution in 1991.

mcqs.q: ASIS (Australian Secret Intelligence Service) belongs to:
mcqs.explanation: ASIS (Australian Secret Intelligence Service) is Australia's foreign intelligence agency, founded in 1952. It is Australia's equivalent of MI6 and CIA. Australia also has ASIO (Australian Security Intelligence Organisation) for domestic counter-intelligence.

mcqs.q: DIGO (Defence Imagery and Geospatial Organisation) operates in:
mcqs.explanation: DIGO (Defence Imagery and Geospatial Organisation) was Australia's military intelligence agency specializing in imagery and geospatial analysis. It has since been renamed the Australian Geospatial-Intelligence Organisation (AGO).

mcqs.q: HNA (Army Intelligence Office) is the military intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: HNA (Heeresnachrichtenamt — Army Intelligence Office) is Austria's military intelligence agency. Austria also has BVT (Federal Agency for State Protection and Counter Terrorism) for domestic security and counter-intelligence.

mcqs.q: MTN (Ministry of National Security) was the intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: MTN (Milli Təhlükəsizlik Nazirliyi — Ministry of National Security) was Azerbaijan's intelligence agency. It has since been reorganized as the SSS (State Security Service — Dövlət Təhlükəsizliyi Xidməti).

mcqs.q: SIB (Security and Intelligence Branch) is the intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: SIB (Security and Intelligence Branch) is the Bahamas' intelligence and security organization. As a small Caribbean nation, the Bahamas maintains a modest intelligence apparatus focused primarily on counter-narcotics and border security.

mcqs.q: DGFI (Directorate General of Forces Intelligence) is the military intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: DGFI (Directorate General of Forces Intelligence) is Bangladesh's military intelligence agency, under the Bangladesh Army. Bangladesh's civilian intelligence agency is NSI (National Security Intelligence), under the Home Ministry.

mcqs.q: NSI (National Security Intelligence) is the primary civilian intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: NSI (National Security Intelligence) is Bangladesh's primary civilian intelligence agency, under the Home Ministry. For military intelligence, Bangladesh uses DGFI (Directorate General of Forces Intelligence) under the armed forces.

mcqs.q: ADIV/SGRS (General Intelligence and Security Service) operates in:
mcqs.explanation: ADIV/SGRS (Algemene Dienst Inlichting en Veiligheid / Service Général du Renseignement et de la Sécurité) is Belgium's military intelligence agency. Belgium's civilian intelligence is VSSE (State Security Service).

mcqs.q: OSA (Intelligence-Security Agency) is the intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: OSA (Obavještajno-sigurnosna agencija — Intelligence-Security Agency) is the primary intelligence agency of Bosnia and Herzegovina, created in 2004 to handle both civilian and military intelligence.

mcqs.q: ABIN is the national intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: ABIN (Agência Brasileira de Inteligência — Brazilian Intelligence Agency) is Brazil's national intelligence agency, created in 1999. It is responsible for protecting Brazilian state interests domestically and abroad.

mcqs.q: NRS (National Intelligence Service) is the foreign intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: NRS (Nacionalna Razuznavatelna Sluzhba — National Intelligence Service) is Bulgaria's foreign intelligence agency. Bulgaria's domestic security is handled by DANS (State Agency for National Security).

mcqs.q: CSE (Communications Security Establishment) is the signals intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: CSE (Communications Security Establishment) is Canada's signals intelligence (SIGINT) agency, equivalent to the NSA (USA) and GCHQ (UK). Canada is a member of the Five Eyes intelligence alliance along with USA, UK, Australia, and New Zealand.

mcqs.q: The Canadian Forces Intelligence Command primarily operates in:
mcqs.explanation: The Canadian Forces Intelligence Command (CFINTCOM) is Canada's military intelligence organization, coordinating intelligence for the Canadian Armed Forces. Canada's civilian intelligence is CSIS (Canadian Security Intelligence Service), founded in 1984.

mcqs.q: MSS (Ministry of State Security) is the civilian intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: MSS (Guojia Anquan Bu — Ministry of State Security) is China's primary civilian intelligence agency, founded in 1983. It handles both domestic counter-intelligence and foreign intelligence operations. China's military intelligence is under the PLA's Joint Staff Department.

mcqs.q: ANI (Agencia Nacional de Inteligencia) is the intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: ANI (Agencia Nacional de Inteligencia — National Intelligence Agency) is Chile's national intelligence agency, created in 2004 to replace the former DINE. It handles national security intelligence under civilian control.

mcqs.q: VSOA (Military Security and Intelligence Agency) belongs to:
mcqs.explanation: VSOA (Vojna Sigurnosno-Obavještajna Agencija — Military Security and Intelligence Agency) is Croatia's military intelligence agency. Croatia's civilian intelligence is SOA (Security and Intelligence Agency).

mcqs.q: DGI (Dirección General de Inteligencia) is the state intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: DGI (Dirección General de Inteligencia — General Intelligence Directorate) is Cuba's primary intelligence agency. It is known for operating extensively in Latin America and historically for close ties with Soviet and Russian intelligence.

mcqs.q: BIS (Security Information Service) is the domestic intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: BIS (Bezpečnostní informační služba — Security Information Service) is the Czech Republic's domestic counter-intelligence agency. The Czech foreign intelligence is ÚZSI (Office for Foreign Relations and Information).

mcqs.q: PET (Danish Security and Intelligence Service) is the national intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: PET (Politiets Efterretningstjeneste — Danish Security and Intelligence Service) is Denmark's national intelligence agency, handling domestic security and counter-terrorism. Denmark's foreign/military intelligence is FE (Defence Intelligence Service).

mcqs.q: Al-Mukhabarat al-'Ammah is the General Intelligence Service of:
mcqs.explanation: Al-Mukhabarat al-'Ammah (General Intelligence Service / GIS) is Egypt's primary civilian intelligence agency, headquartered in Cairo. Egypt also has Mukhabarat el-Harbeya for military intelligence. The GIS is one of the most influential agencies in the Arab world.

mcqs.q: Mukhabarat el-Harbeya is the Military Intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: Mukhabarat el-Harbeya is Egypt's military intelligence service, distinct from GIS (General Intelligence Service). It operates under the Egyptian Armed Forces and focuses on military security and strategic intelligence.

mcqs.q: MI5 (Security Service) is the domestic counter-intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: MI5 (Military Intelligence, Section 5), officially the Security Service, is the UK's domestic counter-intelligence and security agency, founded in 1909. Headquartered at Thames House, London, its head is called Director General. MI6 handles foreign intelligence.

mcqs.q: MI6 (Secret Intelligence Service) is the foreign intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: MI6 (Military Intelligence, Section 6), officially the Secret Intelligence Service (SIS), is the UK's foreign intelligence agency, founded in 1909. Headquartered at Vauxhall Cross, London, its chief is traditionally called 'C'. James Bond is a fictional MI6 agent.

mcqs.q: Defence Intelligence (formerly DIS) is a military intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: Defence Intelligence (formerly DIS — Defence Intelligence Staff) is the UK's military intelligence organization under the Ministry of Defence. It provides intelligence to the armed forces and policy makers, alongside civilian agencies MI5 and MI6.

mcqs.q: CIA (Central Intelligence Agency) is the primary foreign intelligence service of:
mcqs.explanation: CIA (Central Intelligence Agency) is the United States' primary foreign intelligence service, founded under the National Security Act of 1947. Headquartered in Langley, Virginia. The CIA handles foreign intelligence; the FBI handles domestic intelligence and law enforcement.

mcqs.q: Which of the following does NOT exist within the 18 official members of the US intelligence community?
mcqs.explanation: There is NO 'Judicial Intelligence Agency' in the US intelligence community. The US IC has 18 official members including CIA, FBI, NSA, DIA, NGA, DEA, and intelligence elements of the military services — but the judiciary is not part of the intelligence community.

mcqs.q: DIA (Defense Intelligence Agency) operates in:
mcqs.explanation: DIA (Defense Intelligence Agency) is the United States' primary military intelligence agency, founded in 1961 and headquartered at the Pentagon, Washington DC. It provides intelligence to the US military and defense policymakers.

mcqs.q: SEBIN (Bolivarian Intelligence Service) is the premier intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: SEBIN (Servicio Bolivariano de Inteligencia Nacional) is Venezuela's premier intelligence agency. It replaced the former DISIP in 2009 under President Hugo Chávez and is known for its extensive political surveillance role.

mcqs.q: DISIP (Directorate of Intelligence and Prevention Services) was the predecessor to SEBIN in:
mcqs.explanation: DISIP (Dirección de los Servicios de Inteligencia y Prevención) was Venezuela's intelligence agency before being replaced by SEBIN (Bolivarian Intelligence Service) in 2009 under President Hugo Chávez.

mcqs.q: KCIA (Korean Central Intelligence Agency) was the former intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: KCIA (Korean Central Intelligence Agency) was South Korea's intelligence agency established in 1961. It was renamed ANSP in 1981 and then reorganized as NIS (National Intelligence Service) in 1999, which is South Korea's current primary intelligence agency.

mcqs.q: ANSP (Agency for National Security Planning) was the former intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: ANSP (Agency for National Security Planning) was South Korea's intelligence agency from 1981 to 1999, formerly known as KCIA (Korean Central Intelligence Agency). It was reorganized as NIS (National Intelligence Service) in 1999.

mcqs.q: SID (Security and Intelligence Division) is the foreign intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: SID (Security and Intelligence Division) is Singapore's foreign intelligence agency. Singapore also has ISD (Internal Security Department) for domestic intelligence. Given Singapore's strategic location, its intelligence services are highly professional.

mcqs.q: General Intelligence Presidency (GIP) is the primary intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: GIP (General Intelligence Presidency), formerly known as Al Mukhabarat Al A'amah, is Saudi Arabia's primary intelligence agency, reporting directly to the King. It handles both domestic and foreign intelligence operations.

mcqs.q: NIA (National Intelligence Agency) is a domestic intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: NIA (National Intelligence Agency) was South Africa's domestic intelligence agency. South Africa's intelligence has since been reorganized under the SSA (State Security Agency), which merged NIA and SASS (foreign intelligence) in 2009.

mcqs.q: CIO (Central Intelligence Organisation) is the national intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: CIO (Central Intelligence Organisation) is Zimbabwe's national intelligence agency, long associated with the ZANU-PF government. It is known for extensive domestic surveillance and political intelligence operations.

mcqs.q: DCRI / DGSI is the internal intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: DCRI (Direction Centrale du Renseignement Intérieur) was France's domestic intelligence agency, renamed DGSI (Direction Générale de la Sécurité Intérieure) in 2014. France's foreign intelligence is DGSE (Direction Générale de la Sécurité Extérieure).

mcqs.q: RAW (Research and Analysis Wing) is the primary foreign intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: RAW (Research and Analysis Wing) is India's primary foreign intelligence agency, founded in 1968 following intelligence failures in the 1965 Indo-Pakistan war. It is under the Cabinet Secretariat and reports directly to the Prime Minister. IMPORTANT: the correct full form is 'Wing' NOT 'Van' — a very common trick question.

mcqs.q: IB (Intelligence Bureau) is Pakistan's oldest civilian intelligence agency, founded in:
mcqs.explanation: Pakistan's IB (Intelligence Bureau) was founded in 1947 at independence, making it Pakistan's oldest civilian intelligence agency. It operates under the Interior Ministry and handles domestic counter-intelligence. Pakistan's premier agency ISI was founded a year later in 1948.

mcqs.q: MI (Military Intelligence) is an intelligence agency belonging to:
mcqs.explanation: MI (Military Intelligence) is Pakistan's army intelligence arm, operating under the Chief of Army Staff (COAS). It is distinct from ISI (which serves all three military services) and focuses specifically on army-related intelligence and security operations.

mcqs.q: ISI (Inter-Services Intelligence) is the premier intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: ISI (Inter-Services Intelligence) is Pakistan's premier intelligence agency, founded in 1948. Headquartered in Islamabad, it serves all three military branches. The Director General is always a serving Lieutenant General. ISI is consistently ranked among the world's top 3 intelligence agencies.

mcqs.q: Gestapo (Secret State Police) was the official secret police of:
mcqs.explanation: Gestapo (Geheime Staatspolizei — Secret State Police) was Nazi Germany's secret police, established in 1933 under Hermann Göring and later Heinrich Himmler. It was responsible for persecuting political opponents, Jews, and resistance fighters during the Holocaust.

mcqs.q: HUR/GUR (Main Directorate of Intelligence) is the military intelligence service of:
mcqs.explanation: HUR (Holovne Upravlinnya Rozvidky — Main Directorate of Intelligence, also known as GUR) is Ukraine's military intelligence agency. Ukraine's civilian intelligence is SBU (Security Service of Ukraine), successor to the Soviet KGB in Ukraine.

mcqs.q: CIB (Criminal Intelligence Bureau) is part of the police force of:
mcqs.explanation: CIB (Criminal Intelligence Bureau) is a specialized unit of the Hong Kong Police Force responsible for criminal intelligence gathering and analysis. As a Special Administrative Region of China, Hong Kong maintains its own law enforcement agencies.

mcqs.q: JFIU (Joint Financial Intelligence Unit) operates in:
mcqs.explanation: JFIU (Joint Financial Intelligence Unit) is Hong Kong's financial intelligence unit, jointly operated by the Hong Kong Police Force and the Customs and Excise Department. It focuses on money laundering and terrorist financing intelligence.

mcqs.q: CBI (Central Bureau of Investigation) is the premier investigating agency of:
mcqs.explanation: CBI (Central Bureau of Investigation) is India's premier federal investigating agency, under the Ministry of Personnel. While primarily a law enforcement body, it is frequently tested in competitive exams alongside RAW (foreign intelligence) and IB (domestic intelligence).

mcqs.q: MNDF (Maldives National Defence Force) is the defence and security force of:
mcqs.explanation: MNDF (Maldives National Defence Force) is the combined armed forces of the Maldives, which also includes intelligence functions. As a small island nation without a separate dedicated intelligence agency, the MNDF handles security intelligence.

mcqs.q: ENI (Escuela Nacional de Inteligencia) is the national intelligence academy of:
mcqs.explanation: ENI (Escuela Nacional de Inteligencia — National Intelligence School) is Argentina's national intelligence training academy. Argentina's primary intelligence agency is AFI (Agencia Federal de Inteligencia), which replaced the former SIDE (Secretaría de Inteligencia).

mcqs.q: DOJ (Directorate of Judicial Surveillance) is an investigative body in:
mcqs.explanation: DOJ (Directorate of Judicial Surveillance) is a judicial investigative body in Argentina. Argentina's national intelligence infrastructure includes multiple agencies across civilian, military, and judicial domains.

mcqs.q: BMM (Mixed Mobile Brigade) was a paramilitary intelligence unit in:
mcqs.explanation: BMM (Brigade Mixte Mobile — Mixed Mobile Brigade) was a paramilitary intelligence unit in Cameroon. Cameroon's primary security service is DGRE (Direction Générale de la Recherche Extérieure) for foreign intelligence.

mcqs.q: BNI (Bureau of National Investigations) is the internal intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: BNI (Bureau of National Investigations) is Ghana's domestic intelligence agency, responsible for internal security and counter-intelligence. Ghana's foreign intelligence is handled by the Research Department.

mcqs.q: VEVAK (Ministry of Intelligence and National Security) is the primary intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: VEVAK (Vezarat-e Ettela'at va Amniat-e Keshvar — Ministry of Intelligence), also called MOIS, is Iran's primary intelligence agency. It replaced SAVAK after the 1979 Islamic Revolution. SAVAK was the Shah-era intelligence service trained partly by CIA and Mossad.

mcqs.q: SAVAK was the Shah-era secret police and intelligence service of:
mcqs.explanation: SAVAK (Sazeman-e Ettela'at va Amniyat-e Keshvar) was Iran's intelligence service under Shah Mohammad Reza Pahlavi, dissolved after the 1979 Islamic Revolution. It was replaced by VEVAK/MOIS. SAVAK was trained partly by the CIA and Mossad during the Shah's era.

mcqs.q: Mazorca was a 19th-century secret police force in:
mcqs.explanation: Mazorca was a secret paramilitary organization and informal secret police in Argentina during the dictatorship of Juan Manuel de Rosas (1835–1852). It was notorious for its use of terror and assassination against political opponents.

mcqs.q: SAKK is an intelligence-related unit associated with which country's security apparatus?
mcqs.explanation: SAKK is associated with Sweden's Security Police (SÄPO). Sweden's primary domestic intelligence agency is SÄPO (Säkerhetspolisen — Security Police), while MUST (Military Intelligence and Security Service) handles Sweden's military intelligence.

mcqs.q: Scotland Yard is the headquarters of the Metropolitan Police Service in:
mcqs.explanation: New Scotland Yard is the headquarters of the Metropolitan Police Service in London, United Kingdom. While not strictly an intelligence agency, it houses the Met's Counter Terrorism Command (SO15) and is frequently referenced in competitive exam questions alongside MI5 and MI6.

mcqs.q: JIC (Joint Intelligence Committee) coordinates intelligence policy in:
mcqs.explanation: JIC (Joint Intelligence Committee) is the United Kingdom's primary mechanism for coordinating intelligence policy across MI5, MI6, GCHQ, and military intelligence. It produces assessments for the Prime Minister and the Cabinet.

mcqs.q: FBI (Federal Bureau of Investigation) handles domestic intelligence and security in:
mcqs.explanation: FBI (Federal Bureau of Investigation) handles domestic intelligence, security, and law enforcement in the United States, founded in 1908. Headquartered in Washington DC under the Department of Justice, its director serves a 10-year term. The CIA handles foreign intelligence — a key distinction for competitive exams.

mcqs.q: FBI Directorate of Intelligence is a branch of the Federal Bureau of Investigation in:
mcqs.explanation: The FBI Directorate of Intelligence is a branch within the US Federal Bureau of Investigation responsible for coordinating intelligence collection and analysis. It was established after 9/11 to improve the FBI's intelligence capabilities alongside its law enforcement role.

mcqs.q: DAS (Administrative Department of Security) was a former intelligence agency in:
mcqs.explanation: DAS (Departamento Administrativo de Seguridad) was Colombia's civilian intelligence agency, dissolved in 2011 after multiple scandals including illegal surveillance. It was replaced by DNI (Dirección Nacional de Inteligencia).

mcqs.q: COSENA is a defense and intelligence coordination council associated with:
mcqs.explanation: COSENA (Consejo de Seguridad Nacional — National Security Council) is Ecuador's defense and intelligence coordination body. Ecuador's national intelligence agency is SENAIN (Secretaría Nacional de Inteligencia).

mcqs.q: DIS (Dirección de Inteligencia y Seguridad) is the national intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: DIS (Dirección de Inteligencia y Seguridad) is Costa Rica's national intelligence agency. Costa Rica is unique in Latin America as a country with no military, but it maintains civilian intelligence services for national security.

mcqs.q: KDS was the communist-era state security service of:
mcqs.explanation: KDS (Komitet za Darzhavna Sigurnost — Committee for State Security) was Bulgaria's communist-era state security service, the Bulgarian equivalent of the Soviet KGB. It was dissolved after communism's fall in 1990 and succeeded by NSS and DANS.

mcqs.q: RSU (Rifles Security Unit) was a paramilitary intelligence unit in:
mcqs.explanation: RSU (Rifles Security Unit) was a paramilitary intelligence unit in Bangladesh, associated with the Bangladesh Rifles (now Bangladesh Border Guards — BGB). Bangladesh's primary intelligence agencies are NSI (civilian) and DGFI (military).

mcqs.q: General Counterintelligence Office is nested under SEBIN in:
mcqs.explanation: Venezuela's General Counterintelligence Office operates under SEBIN (Bolivarian Intelligence Service). SEBIN manages multiple intelligence functions including foreign intelligence, domestic surveillance, and counterintelligence operations.

mcqs.q: The US intelligence community officially comprises how many member agencies?
mcqs.explanation: The United States Intelligence Community officially comprises 18 member agencies, including CIA, FBI, NSA, DIA, NGA, NRO, DEA, and intelligence branches of each military service. This is a commonly tested fact in FPSC, CSS, and ISSB competitive exams.

mcqs.q: OSS (Office of Strategic Services) was the World War II forerunner of the CIA in:
mcqs.explanation: OSS (Office of Strategic Services) was the United States' WWII intelligence organization, founded in 1942 under Major General William Donovan ('Wild Bill'). It was dissolved in 1945 and its functions were largely taken over by the CIA when created by the National Security Act of 1947.

mcqs.q: Which Act created the CIA and established the modern US national security structure?
mcqs.explanation: The National Security Act of 1947, signed by President Harry S. Truman, created the CIA (Central Intelligence Agency), the NSC (National Security Council), and unified the Department of Defense. This is a standard FPSC, PPSC, and CSS MCQ.

mcqs.q: The Security Accreditation Board (SAB) is the security authority for the:
mcqs.explanation: The Security Accreditation Board (SAB) is the security authority for the EU Space Programme (formerly Galileo satellite system). It is responsible for accrediting space systems and ground infrastructure to meet EU security standards.

mcqs.q: SIPA (State Investigation and Protection Agency) is a law enforcement agency in:
mcqs.explanation: SIPA (Državna agencija za istrage i zaštitu — State Investigation and Protection Agency) is Bosnia and Herzegovina's state-level law enforcement agency, handling serious crimes, war crimes, terrorism, and organized crime investigations.

mcqs.q: PST (Police Security Service) is the police intelligence agency of:
mcqs.explanation: PST (Politiets Sikkerhetstjeneste — Police Security Service) is Norway's police intelligence agency, responsible for counter-terrorism, counter-espionage, and extremism prevention. Norway's military intelligence is E-tjenesten (Etterretningstjenesten).

mcqs.q: NSM (National Security Authority) is the preventive security agency of:
mcqs.explanation: NSM (Nasjonal sikkerhetsmyndighet — National Security Authority) is Norway's national preventive security agency, responsible for protecting critical infrastructure, classified information systems, and advising on cyber security. It is distinct from PST (police intelligence) and E-tjenesten (military intelligence).
